display_name: IPv6 needs a working link-local address — a configured global address is not enough for ND or routing
context: An interface has a correct global IPv6 address configured but neighbors will not resolve and IPv6 routing adjacencies over the link do not form
observation: Global IPv6 address is present and 'looks right', yet all neighbors are INCOMPLETE and OSPFv3/IS-IS/BGP-over-link-local sessions never come up
naive_interpretation: The global addressing or the routing protocol is misconfigured
correct_interpretation: Every operational IPv6 interface needs a link-local (fe80::/10) address, and it does almost all the work: ND sources every NS/NA/RS from the link-local, routers source RAs from it, and IPv6 routing protocols use link-local next-hops (OSPFv3 and IS-IS adjacencies, and eBGP/iBGP sessions peered to fe80:: addresses). If the link-local is absent (IPv6 not actually enabled on the interface) or itself failed DAD (which disables IPv6 on the interface), then no ND happens at all — neighbors never resolve, RAs are neither sent nor accepted, and routing adjacencies never form — even though the global address is configured and visible. The global address is a red herring; verify a valid (non-tentative, non-duplicate) fe80:: address exists first.
why: ND messages are sourced from the link-local address, so without it the node cannot solicit or answer neighbors
OSPFv3/IS-IS form adjacencies using link-local addresses, and BGP commonly peers over fe80:: — a missing link-local breaks these regardless of global addressing
A link-local that failed DAD disables IPv6 on the whole interface, producing the same symptom as never enabling IPv6

confirmation_commands.purpose: Confirm a valid fe80:: link-local is listed (not TENTATIVE/DUPLICATE)
Look for 'IPv6 is enabled, link-local address is fe80::...'
Verify the link-local line is present and valid
Confirm a fe80:: link-local on the interface

traps: Focusing on the global address while the interface has no usable link-local
Assuming 'ipv6 address <global>' alone enables IPv6 everywhere — if no link-local is derived/valid, ND and routing still fail
Missing that a duplicate link-local (DAD failure) silently disabled IPv6 on the interface
Peering BGP/OSPFv3 to a link-local without realizing a missing fe80:: is the root cause

sources.title: RFC 4861 — Neighbor Discovery for IPv6 (neighbor cache states 7.3.2)
